assessment.instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Raise both arms straight overhead, biceps by your ears. Slowly squat down as far as comfortable. Note where you feel limited: ankles, hips, upper back, or shoulders.
assessment.what_it_reveals: The overhead squat reveals your body's priority compensation pattern. Heels rising = ankle mobility. Knees caving = hip weakness. Arms falling forward = thoracic stiffness. The first thing that breaks down is your starting point.
assessment.cta: Whatever broke down first in your overhead squat, the exercises in today's video are designed to address that exact limitation.

strategy_json.theme_narrative: This week 'fibromyalgia' is trending across search, social, and health forums. Based on the data we collected, this is a high-opportunity topic for Bart's audience of desk workers, athletes, and chronic-pain patients.

monday_video.title: Fibromyalgia — 4 Exercises That Actually Work (2026)
monday_video.hook: 'Fibromyalgia' searches are surging right now. Most advice online is wrong. Here's what actually works based on 15,000 hours of clinical experience.
talking_points: Open with the data: 'fibromyalgia' is trending because...
Explain the biomechanics: here's what's happening in the body
Common mistakes: what most people do that makes it worse
The fix: walk through 4 exercises with form cues
monday_video.end_cta: Try these today and tell me in the comments how you feel by Wednesday.

ai_findability_tips: Use Q&A format: 'What causes fibromyalgia?' with a direct answer paragraph so LLMs and featured snippets can extract it
Include structured data (HowTo + FAQPage schema) for search engines and AI crawlers
Write definitive statements LLMs can quote as authoritative — e.g. 'The most effective exercise for fibromyalgia is...'

engagement_replies.suggested_reply: Great question. From my clinical experience with fibromyalgia, the most common mistake I see is jumping straight to stretching without addressing the underlying movement pattern. Try starting with Gentle walking — it targets the root cause rather than just the symptom.
